description: This scenario models a high-impact social instability event characterized by widespread unrest, institutional distrust, and societal fragmentation, triggering cascading effects across governance, economic systems, and cyber infrastructure.

cyber_stability.description: Digital systems affected by misinformation, cyber incidents, and communication breakdown

ai_governance.description: AI governance pressured by emergency measures and misinformation control

legal_coherence.description: Legal inconsistencies due to emergency enforcement and institutional strain

economic_resilience.description: Economic slowdown due to instability, disruptions, and reduced productivity

social_stability.description: Severe unrest, protests, institutional distrust, and social fragmentation

shock_propagation.propagation_pattern: social unrest → economic disruption → legal fragmentation → cyber instability

cyber_stability.impact: Increased cyber incidents and communication disruptions

social_stability.impact: Severe societal instability and trust breakdown

decision_integrity_score.impact: System shifts from stable to moderate-high risk state

recovery_dynamics.recovery_pattern: social stabilization → institutional trust rebuilding → economic normalization → governance stabilization

uncertainty_model.notes: Outcome variability depends heavily on governance response and public trust dynamics

simulation_notes.purpose: Evaluates systemic impact of social instability across sovereign domains
simulation_notes.interpretation: Highlights social system as a critical trigger of cross-domain instability
simulation_notes.usage: Public policy design, crisis management, and institutional resilience planning
